MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-18-2024 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 525

DISCOVERY 525, DEFENDANT RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION OR ALTERNATIVELY, TO PRECLUDE PLAINTIFF FROM TESTIFYING AT TRIAL. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION, AND TO PRODUCE THOSE DOCUMENTS IDENTIFIED IN THE MAY 28, 2024 RENOTICE OF HIS DEPOSITION, IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS DIRECTED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER AND SUBMIT IT TO PSW@HASSARD.COM FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. IN VIEW OF THE FACT THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR JUNE OF NEXT YEAR, THE ORDER SHALL DIRECT COUNSEL FOR THE PARTIES TO MEET AND AGREE UPON A MUTUALLY CONVENIENT DEPOSITION DATE NO LATER THAN JANUARY 31, 2025. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURTS RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM PHILIP WARD AT PSW@HASSARD.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: PHILIP WARD; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (525/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition Or Alternatively, To Preclude Plaintiff From Testifying At Trial; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition Or Alternatively, To Preclude Plaintiff From Testifying At Trial

Defendant has filed a motion for an order compelling plaintiff to appear for deposition or, in the alternative, for an order barring plaintiff from testifying at trial. In response, plaintiff's counsel filed a statement of non-opposition.

Accordingly, for good cause shown, defendant's motion for an order compelling plaintiff to appear for deposition, and to produce those documents identified in the May 28, 2024 renotice of his deposition, is GRANTED. Counsel for defendant is directed to prepare a proposed form of order and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature. In view of the fact the trial date is set for June of next year, the order shall direct counsel for the parties to meet and agree upon a mutually convenient deposition date no later than January 31, 2025

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Courts ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Philip ward at psw@hassard.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)
